steps.title: Set up the area equation
steps.content: The base is 10 cm greater than the height, so if the height is $h$, the base is $b = h + 10$. Using the triangle area formula with Area = 300: $$300 = \frac{1}{2}(h+10) \cdot h$$

steps.title: Backsolve the choices
steps.content:
The answer choices are simple integers, so we can test them directly.

Backsolving by testing the candidate (30): If $h = 30$, then $b = 30 + 10 = 40$, and Area $= \frac{1}{2}(40)(30) = 600 \neq 300$. Fails.

Testing this candidate (20): If $h = 20$, then $b = 20 + 10 = 30$, and Area $= \frac{1}{2}(30)(20) = \frac{600}{2} = 300$. This matches. That matches the correct answer: B is correct.
